5 min read
In Part 3 of this module, we’re going to discuss link triggers — the
“doorways” into your SPP Stacks, including your various Soap
Opera Series. Link triggers operate the same way for both, the
only difference is the context that frames the link trigger.
Our de�nition of a “doorway” is made up of three components:
context + active intent + timing.
As described in our Bridge Email module, context is the framing
of an opportunity. The point is not to attract the most clicks, but to
attract the most relevant clicks. The whole purpose of a Bridge
Email is embodied in: context + active intent + timing.
The goal is to have these three things in alignment, and for that
alignment to result in a “click” to learn more, or to be part of a
promotion (enrollment). This setup creates the perfect
conditions for an evergreen promotion.
The beauty of this work�ow is that it can create doorways into
your evergreen promotions from pretty much anywhere
accessible through email.
Story-Powered Promotions
(SPPs) — Part 3
www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 10 --

▪ RBS (the �nal email can be either a soft of hard bridge),
▪ Within the narrative of an SOS,
▪ Within your Value Newsletter,
▪ Email template links,
▪ PS links.
Creating a link trigger is very simple. But �rst, we need two
landing pages.
Step 1 (Redirect Pages)
The �rst step is to create two pages — one when the link trigger is
clicked (and tag added), and one when someone clicks to be
removed from a promotion (another tag added).
Every link trigger needs a page to redirect to. We have two generic
pages we use for this. This is super handy because we use these
for almost all our SPP and PLS link triggers.
▪ https://tinylittlebusinesses.com/segmented/
▪ https://tinylittlebusinesses.com/muted/
Be creative. This is an opportunity to delight people, to put a smile
on their face. Nuances matter. Wistia has a great video version
here. Derek Sivers, when he ran CD Baby, had a brilliant one.
Here’s what it said.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 10 --

Step 2 (Link Trigger)
Every decent ESP will have a version of a Link Trigger (LT). We’ll
show how to create a LT in ConvertKit. The process will be similar
elsewhere.
There are a few ways to do this in ConvertKit, so we’ll show you
our preferred way.
Dashboard Automate Rules New Rule
Rules have a Trigger (in our case, clicks a link) and an Action (in
our case, add a tag).
For the Trigger, select Clicks a Link, and enter the destination URL
(for use, we use our ‘segmented’ page cited above) and give the
link a name.
Examples:
▪ (PLS) Interested - LBC - Nov 27-30 (Black Friday 2018)
▪ (SPP) SPP - AoE (via VNL Footer)
For the Action, select Add Tag, then select the tag you want added
when the link is clicked. In the case of an SPP, it’ll be the SPP Stack
tag.
Examples:
www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 10 --

▪ 	SPP - AoE
▪ 	SPP - TTE
▪ 	SPP - SOI
▪ 	SPP - TDB
Repeat the process for the “mute” Link Trigger:
▪ (PLS) Mute - LBC - Nov 27-30 (Black Friday 2018)
▪ (SPP) Mute - SPP - AoE
Now you’ll have two Link Triggers: one to add someone to an SPP
Stack, and one to “mute” someone from continuing to receive a
promotion.
The latter is not a requirement, but in our world of emphasizing a
relationship-�rst posture, is it. Do what feels right for you.
If you need a more detailed ‘how to’ for doing this in ConvertKit,
here’s a link to their help page — How To Use Link Triggers.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 10 --

Step 3 (Triggering a Link Trigger)
In ConvertKit, accessing a link trigger is easy, whether from the
new editor or legacy one (our preference).
Select the text you want to turn into an active intent “hand raise”
link, then select the Link Trigger rule you created in the previous
step.
Done. It’s as simple as that.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 10 --

Now let’s zoom out and explore link triggers — our “doorways” —
from a 10,000′ view.
10,000′ View of Doorways into SPPs
Let’s unpack this illustration which represents many variations,
expressed in this single example.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 10 --

The email itself could be sent as part of an automated series (end
of an RBS, or within the narrative of an SOS), or as a broadcast,
which would be a solo Hard Bridge, or within the context of a
VNL.
In this example, the email would supply the context for the active
intent link trigger. SPP Stack (A) represents a link trigger that
directly triggers an SPP Stack.
Boom, promo starts.
The P.S. is another spot where a link trigger doorway can live. In
this example, it could be a second opportunity to trigger the SPP
Stack (A) promotion.
Now let’s consider what’s happening in the footer of the page.
This could be hard coded right into the email template, meaning
these footer link would be present from EVERY email sent where
this template is used.
For this example, let’s presume our footer contained this text:
▪ The Durable Business — $0 to $100K
▪ Sphere of In�uence — Make Better Prospects
www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 10 --

▪ Art of Email — Relationship-Driven Modern Email Marketing
▪ The Tra�c Engine — Better Leads Matter
▪ This email has been lovingly handcrafted and sent with the
awesome ConvertKit service.
It’s completely unrelated to the body of the email, including any
context expressed within it. However, the links themselves
provide enough context to give us a clue as to someone’s intent.
These are not “blind” links.
That said, it would be dumb in our opinion to have these links
going directly to an SPP Stack to launch an evergreen promotion.
That would be very jarring for someone.
Instead, the �rst four links could be “doorways” to automated
Hard Bridge emails.
Example:
Hey FNAME,
We noticed you clicked a link to check out our product, The Durable
Business. So, we wanted to ask whether you would like to raise your hand
to hear more about it.
We have a six-part series (over �ve days) that’ll unpack what we teach
within TDB and how that value may be something that �ts a need you
have.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 10 --

NOTE: In the example above, the ‘click here’ link wouldn’t directly
trigger an SPP Stack (although it could). In AoE we have a new
kind of SOS we call a “Showcase Series.” It’s not a separate thing,
instead it’s one of many expressions of an SOS. A Showcase Series
doesn’t explicitly sell, it “showcases” a product, like we did for
TBD in 2020 here (which was a �ve-part series, archived on that
single page).
Although a Showcase Series doesn’t explicitly sell, it creates the
conditions that channels desire towards the product. In many
cases, the sale happens organically without any need for a capital
P, Promotion.
That said, a Showcase Series can be bookended with an SPP Stack,
like this:
If you would like us to send you the series, click here, and we’ll send you the
�rst email right away.
Best, NAME
www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 10 --

The Hard Bridge (HB) on the far left, in this example, is the email
footer link from the previous email. The HB is us respecting
someone’s attention, asking for their active intent, and only then
sending them the SOS-powered Showcase Series.
All of this — everything — is completely automated. It’ll work every
day, every moment a subscriber interacts with our world and
clicks links that matter to them.
When you internalize the power of evergreen marketing, as
expressed within AoE, it’ll become the primary “workhorse” of
developing deeper relationships, and when “perfect” non-linear
moments exist, revenue is generated.
Please ask questions and leave comments below.
—André & Shawn
www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 10 --
